assertions: A1: Follows a structured multi-step recon flow
A2: Uses playwright-cli commands (not Playwright MCP, not CDP, not browser DevTools manually)
A3: Produces a structured RECON-REPORT.md (with sections for architecture, API, protections, strategy)
A4: Has specific eval commands for SSR framework detection (Next.js __NEXT_DATA__, Nuxt, etc.)
A5: Mentions the Force SPA Navigation trick
A6: Includes a protection detection script/eval (checks Cloudflare, CAPTCHA, WAF)
A8: Uses a priority chain (API first, HTML scraping last resort)
A9: Identifies specific API endpoints for the target site
A10: Recommends a specific capture strategy with rationale
A11: Maps the strategy to CLI generation impact (what kind of client.py to generate)
A12: Flags warnings or risks (rate limits, token expiry, protection concerns)

A1.evidence: Clear 5-step flow: 1.1 Open & Observe, 1.2 Framework Detection, 1.3 Network Traffic, 1.4 Protection Assessment, 1.5 Generate Report

A2.evidence: All commands use 'npx @playwright/cli@latest -s=recon' syntax consistently (open, snapshot, eval, click, type, tracing-start/stop)

A3.evidence: Section 3 contains a fully structured RECON-REPORT.md with Site Architecture, API Surface, Endpoints Discovered, Protections, robots.txt, Recommended Strategy, Warnings sections

A4.evidence: Step 1.2 has specific eval commands for __NEXT_DATA__, self.__next_f.push (App Router), __NUXT__, __remixContext, __sveltekit_data, __INITIAL_STATE__

A5.evidence: Step 1.3 explicitly uses tracing-start then clicks through internal links to trigger client-side API calls — this is the Force SPA Navigation trick in practice

A6.evidence: Step 1.4 has an all-in-one protection eval checking Cloudflare, CAPTCHA, Akamai, DataDome, PerimeterX, rate limits, fingerprinting, plus a separate detailed Cloudflare cookie/Turnstile check

A7.evidence: Step 1.4 includes 'npx @playwright/cli@latest -s=recon open https://suno.com/robots.txt' followed by snapshot

A8.evidence: Section 4 explicitly states 'API-first' strategy with decision tree: 'API endpoints found in trace? YES -> API-first'. Alternatives table rejects HTML scraping, RSC extraction, GraphQL

A9.evidence: Lists specific endpoints: GET /api/feed/, GET /api/trending/, POST /api/generate/v2/, GET /api/clip/{id}/, GET /api/playlist/{id}/, POST /api/generate/lyrics/, GET /api/billing/info/ with base URL studio-api.suno.ai

A10.evidence: Section 4: 'Strategy: API-First (Standard Trace)' with decision path and explicit rationale comparing REST API vs HTML scraping vs RSC extraction

A11.evidence: Section 4 maps to specific generated files: auth.py (Clerk login + keyring), client.py (httpx with delays), commands/generate.py, commands/feed.py, commands/explore.py, commands/download.py, models.py

A12.evidence: 5 explicit warnings: Clerk JWT expiry, audio URL expiry, rate limits on generation, Cloudflare on API subdomain, robots.txt Disallow /api/

A1.evidence: 5-section flow: Framework/Architecture, API Endpoint Discovery, Anti-Bot Protections, Recommended Strategy, Summary/Predictions

A2.evidence: Uses curl, Chrome DevTools Network tab, CDP (--remote-debugging-port=9222), grep — NOT playwright-cli commands. Playwright is mentioned only as an 'automation layer' in a tools table, never as the primary command interface

A3.evidence: No RECON-REPORT.md is produced. The document itself is a recon report but does not follow the structured template (no explicit 'RECON-REPORT.md' artifact with the required section headers)

A4.evidence: Section 1 mentions checking __NEXT_DATA__, _next/static/chunks/ paths, __clerk_*, __next DOM node, buildId — but via curl/grep rather than eval commands

A5.evidence: No mention of the Force SPA Navigation trick. Phase 2 describes manual browsing in Chrome DevTools but does not frame it as forcing client-side navigation to reveal hidden APIs

A6.evidence: Section 3 has detailed protection detection: checks CF-Ray, cf-cache-status, Cloudflare challenge pages, CAPTCHA, 401/403/429 patterns, headless browser detection

A7.evidence: Section 3 includes full robots.txt analysis with actual content (Allow: /, Disallow: /profile/*/followers, etc.) and sitemaps

A8.evidence: Section 4 recommends 'Authenticated Browser Capture with Token Harvesting' prioritizing API capture. Phase 1 is static analysis, Phase 2 is authenticated traffic capture for API endpoints

A9.evidence: Section 2 lists specific endpoints: POST /api/generate/v2/, GET /api/feed/, GET /api/feed/v2, GET /api/clips/get_songs_by_ids, GET /api/user/user_config/, GET /api/discover, POST /api/extend_audio, POST /api/generate_stems, etc. with request payload examples

A10.evidence: Section 4: 'Authenticated Browser Capture with Token Harvesting' with clear rationale about short-lived Clerk JWTs requiring browser-based token harvest

A11.evidence: Section 4 includes Python code example for auth.py pattern and Section 5 predicts CLI command surface (suno generate, suno feed, suno get, etc.)

A12.evidence: Section 4 Risks table: Token expiry (60s JWT), CAPTCHA on generation, rate limiting, API schema changes, domain migration

A1.evidence: Clear multi-step structure: 1.1 Open/observe, 1.2 Pages vs App Router, 1.3 Rule out competing frameworks, 1.4 Protection check, then sections for __NEXT_DATA__ extraction, Force SPA Navigation, RECON-REPORT, Strategy

A2.evidence: All commands use 'npx @playwright/cli@latest -s=recon' syntax (open, snapshot, eval, click, tracing-start/stop)

A3.evidence: Section 4 contains a full RECON-REPORT.md with Site Architecture, API Surface, Protections, Recommended Strategy, Warnings

A5.evidence: Section 3 is entirely dedicated to 'Force SPA Navigation Trick — Discovering Hidden API Endpoints' with full command sequence and explanation of why initial SSR load hides API calls

A6.evidence: Step 1.4 has the all-in-one protection eval checking Cloudflare, CAPTCHA, Akamai, DataDome, PerimeterX, rate limits, fingerprinting

A7.evidence: Step 1.4 includes robots.txt check via 'npx @playwright/cli@latest -s=recon open https://vercel.com/robots.txt' + snapshot

A8.evidence: Section 5 explicitly prioritizes: 'Start with SSR+API Hybrid, escalate to API-first if Vercel REST endpoints appear'. Summary table shows API-first preferred over SSR data routes

A10.evidence: Section 5: 'SSR+API Hybrid vs API-First' with detailed reasoning about why pure API-first fails (SSR hides calls) and when to escalate

A11.evidence: Section 5 maps to CLI file structure: auth.py, client.py, commands/ (projects.py, deployments.py, settings.py), utils/build_id.py with data fetching priority order

A12.evidence: RECON-REPORT Warnings: buildId changes on every redeploy, session cookie auth, Vercel public API may be more reliable. Also notes Cloudflare and rate limits

A1.evidence: 4-phase structured approach: Phase 0 (Pre-auth), Phase 1 (Auth analysis), Phase 2 (SSR extraction), Phase 3 (API discovery), Phase 4 (CLI plan)

A2.evidence: Uses curl, grep, Chrome DevTools Network tab, node CLI for manifest parsing — NOT playwright-cli. No playwright-cli commands anywhere in the document

A3.evidence: No RECON-REPORT.md artifact is produced. The document is itself a report but does not generate a separate structured RECON-REPORT.md with the required sections

A4.evidence: Section 1 covers __NEXT_DATA__ detection extensively, __NEXT_P, window.next, _next/static/ paths, x-nextjs-cache header. Section 2 details extraction of __NEXT_DATA__ structure

A5.evidence: Does not explicitly mention or describe the Force SPA Navigation trick. Approach A in Section 3 describes 'Network tab interception during user flows' but does not frame it as forcing client-side navigation to reveal hidden APIs that SSR hides

A6.evidence: Section 3 Approach C mentions robots.txt. Section 4 Step 0.4 checks response headers. But there is NO dedicated protection detection script/eval checking for Cloudflare, CAPTCHA, WAF, PerimeterX, etc. Protection is barely addressed

A7.evidence: Section 3 Approach C: 'curl https://vercel.com/robots.txt' is explicitly listed

A8.evidence: Section 4 Phase 4 lists priority order: 1) /_next/data/ SSR endpoints, 2) Direct /api/ endpoints, 3) Full page HTML parse as fallback

A9.evidence: Lists /_next/data/<buildId>/dashboard.json, /api/v1/ or /api/v2/ REST endpoints, /api/graphql possibility, _buildManifest.js for route enumeration

A10.evidence: Key Findings Summary recommends SSR data as primary extraction vector, with layered API discovery approach

A11.evidence: Phase 4 maps to CLI architecture: auth module with credentials.json, data fetching strategy with priority order, build ID management with auto-refresh, command surface (projects list, deployments list, etc.)

A12.evidence: Finding 3: buildId changes on every deploy requiring auto-detection. Mentions auth session management and cache behavior

A1.evidence: Clear multi-step flow: 1.1 Open/observe, 1.2 Framework detection, 1.3 Protection detection, 1.4 Cloudflare check, 1.5 CAPTCHA checks, 1.6 PerimeterX check, 1.7 Network trace, then robots.txt, rate limits, RECON-REPORT, Strategy

A2.evidence: All commands use 'npx @playwright/cli@latest -s=recon' syntax throughout (open, snapshot, eval, click, tracing-start/stop)

A3.evidence: Section 4 contains a structured RECON-REPORT.md with Site Architecture, API Surface, Protections, API Endpoint Candidates, robots.txt Findings, Recommended Strategy, Warnings

A4.evidence: Step 1.2 has eval commands for __NEXT_DATA__ and __NUXT__ detection

A5.evidence: No explicit mention of the Force SPA Navigation trick. Step 1.7 does tracing with clicks but does not frame it as the SPA navigation trick for discovering hidden APIs

A6.evidence: Steps 1.3-1.6 have comprehensive protection evals: all-in-one check (Cloudflare, CAPTCHA, Akamai, DataDome, PerimeterX, fingerprinting), detailed Cloudflare check, individual CAPTCHA type checks (reCAPTCHA v2/v3, hCaptcha, Turnstile), dedicated PerimeterX check

A7.evidence: Section 2 has dedicated robots.txt check with playwright-cli open + snapshot and extraction guidance

A8.evidence: Section 5 recommends 'Protected-Manual with API-First Upgrade Path' — if REST endpoints found, upgrade to API-first; if absent, fall back to HTML extraction

A9.evidence: API endpoints are listed as speculative placeholders: 'likely /api/players', 'likely /api/player/{id}'. RECON-REPORT has '[Fill in after running tracing-start]'. No confirmed endpoints identified

A10.evidence: Section 5: 'Protected-Manual with API-First Upgrade Path' with detailed reasoning about high-value data target + known protection stack

A11.evidence: Section 5 specifies generated client.py requirements: cookie persistence, 2s delays, exponential backoff, Retry-After respect, Chrome UA, rate limit logging. Also describes pause-and-prompt auth step for PerimeterX

A12.evidence: 5 explicit warnings: PerimeterX severity, Cloudflare delays, rate limits on pricing, robots.txt constraints, live pricing cache behavior. Plus 'Not CLI-suitable' fallback recommendation

A1.evidence: 8-section structured report: Cloudflare/WAF, Rate Limiting, robots.txt, Additional Bot Detection, Data Architecture, Protection Strategy Assessment, Strategy if Protected, Summary

A2.evidence: Uses Chrome DevTools MCP + WebFetch as stated in header. JavaScript evaluation via browser console, not playwright-cli commands. No playwright-cli usage anywhere

A3.evidence: No separate RECON-REPORT.md artifact produced. The document is a report but lacks the structured template with dedicated Architecture, API, Protections, Strategy sections under that name

A4.evidence: No SSR framework detection evals. No checks for __NEXT_DATA__, __NUXT__, __remixContext, etc. The report correctly identifies the site as HTML-only/HTMX but does not run the standard framework detection commands

A5.evidence: No mention of the Force SPA Navigation trick anywhere in the document

A6.evidence: Extensive live protection detection: confirmed Cloudflare (CF-Ray, __cfBeacon, challenge-platform oneshot), StatCounter bot detection, FullStory, navigator.webdriver check. Much more detailed than a template script but based on live observation rather than a reusable eval

A7.evidence: Section 3: Full robots.txt analysis with actual content — Disallow rules, privileged crawlers (Googlebot), sitemaps (46 sub-sitemaps), strategic implications

A8.evidence: Section 5.3 states 'No dedicated JSON/REST API found — all data is server-rendered HTML'. Strategy defaults to HTML scraping tiers (stealth browser, sitemap crawling, HTMX fragments). No API-first priority chain — because no API exists, but the priority chain methodology is not articulated

A9.evidence: Section 5.1 lists confirmed URL patterns: /26/players?page=N, /26/player/{id}/{slug}, /26/player/chemistry-link-fragment, /26/comments/threads, /26/player/{id}/price-history. These are HTML endpoints but are specific and confirmed

A10.evidence: Section 7: 5-tier strategy from stealth browser automation (preferred) through sitemap crawling, HTMX fragments, cache exploitation, to CAPTCHA solving fallback

A11.evidence: Section 7 Tier 1 specifies playwright-stealth, persistent Chrome profile, --disable-blink-features flag, realistic delays. Ties to CLI generation approach for scraping-based client

A12.evidence: Section 6 risk summary: StatCounter detects automation, Cloudflare telemetry collection, sustained scraping triggers bot score. Section 4 flags navigator.webdriver as root cause. robots.txt blocks query-string URLs

rows: A11: Maps strategy to CLI generation

key_observations: With-skill runs consistently use playwright-cli commands (A2) — all 3 pass. Without-skill runs never do — all 3 fail. This is the sharpest differentiator.
With-skill runs always produce a structured RECON-REPORT.md artifact (A3). Without-skill runs produce report-like documents but not the structured template.

The Force SPA Navigation trick (A5) is the weakest assertion even with skill — only 2/3 with-skill runs pass. The protected-site with-skill run does tracing+clicks but doesn't name or explain the trick.

Without-skill runs compensate with deeper domain knowledge: Suno without-skill has more detailed API endpoints (actual payload examples), Protected without-skill has live Cloudflare evidence (CF-Ray values, StatCounter bot detection). But they lack the systematic recon methodology.
Protection detection (A6) is strong across all runs except NextJS-without-skill, which barely addresses protections at all.
robots.txt (A7) is the only assertion that passes universally across all 6 runs.

The protected-site with-skill run loses points on A9 (speculative endpoints, not confirmed) because it is a plan rather than executed recon. The without-skill run actually executed the recon and found real endpoints.
